title: Ecbolium lineare
intro: Ecbolium lineare, also known as the Indian spikenard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ecbolium lineare include diterpenoids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ecbolium lineare is often used in the form of essential oils, extracts, and decoctions, which are used to treat anxiety and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Ecbolium lineare belongs to the family Acanthaceae. It is a species of flowering plant. Ecbolium lineare is native to tropical Africa. The plant is known for its linear leaves and produces purple flowers. It is classified as a herbaceous plant, which is often found in dry areas.
